vulnerability.reference: High trust in LLM outputs combined with lack of fact-checking enables dissemination of false information.
vulnerability.generated: Users treat LLM outputs as authoritative without the domain expertise to detect recency-skewed factual errors.

impact.reference: Misinformation erodes trust, leading to uninformed decisions and potential real-world harm.
impact.generated: Misinformation adoption leads to flawed downstream decisions in knowledge-intensive tasks.

dose_response.reference: Recency-biased retrieval in memory-augmented LLMs causes factual inaccuracies; when information consumers use QA tools with high trust and no fact-checking, this enables misinformation dissemination, eroding trust and causing uninformed decisions.
dose_response.generated: Human-inspired memory mechanisms causing systematic prediction errors, through information consumers using LLM-powered QA or summarization tools, trigger users treating outputs as authoritative without detecting recency-skewed errors, leading to misinformation adoption and flawed knowledge-intensive decisions.
